sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this NES title

special_mechanics.name: Side-view platforming sections
special_mechanics.description: Game transitions to side-view areas similar to Rush 'n Attack where Snake walks, jumps, or crawls on stomach while avoiding detection. Movement is limited to knife or handgun as standard weapons.

special_mechanics.name: Underwater crawling
special_mechanics.description: In certain side-view areas, Snake must crawl underwater and requires oxygen tanks to proceed without sustaining damage. Each time oxygen gauge depletes, an equipped tank is consumed automatically.

special_mechanics.name: Melee attack system
special_mechanics.description: B button executes melee attacks (punch or knife depending on assignment). Knife instantly kills guards at close range; punch-defeated guards occasionally drop ammo boxes or rations.

special_mechanics.name: Alert/Combat phase
special_mechanics.description: When discovered by enemy, game enters alert phase causing soldiers to attack. Player must defeat certain number of soldiers to return to infiltration phase, or escape to adjacent screen if only single exclamation mark appears.

tier_notes: Tier 3 empirical/qualitative data. No precise movement physics parameters are documented in available sources. Documentation focuses on gameplay mechanics, level design, and plot rather than technical physics implementation. As an NES title from 1990, precise sub-pixel and physics data would require ROM disassembly not provided in these sources.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Snake's Revenge
